So the Strait of Hormuz is completely blockaded and oil just hit $132 a barrel. You know, that narrow little waterway where 20% of the world's oil normally flows like it's no big deal.

Naturally, shipping companies are now paying 5% of their entire ship's value just for war risk insurance. Because apparently floating through a potential war zone requires slightly more coverage than your Honda Civic.
Oh good, and now fertilizer prices are up 40% right during planting season. The EIA has a whole page about how Gulf supplies just vanished, which is definitely what farmers needed right now.

But wait, it gets better. Qatar's helium plants are offline, so the $611 billion semiconductor industry is having a moment. Because of course your iPhone depends on the same region that's currently exploding.
March inflation jumps to 3.3% and everyone's acting surprised. Like we didn't just watch the global supply chain have a complete nervous breakdown in real time.
And now my coffee costs $9.60 a pound. NINE SIXTY. For COFFEE. I don't care if there's a whole war happening, this is completely unacceptable and frankly the final straw.
